English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
International Federation for Home Economics-United States records
International Federation for Home Economics-United States
IFHE-US
International Federation for Home Economics
International Federation for Home Economics. Region of the Americas
American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences
American Home Economics Association
6760
5.4 cubic feet.
1975-2016. Correspondence, reports, minutes, conference proceedings, photographs, videotapes. Collection material in English Correspondence, reports, and newsletters of IFHE-US; reports, proceedings, photographs, and other conference materials from IFHE Council meetings and Congresses. Records of the IFHE-US Development Fund. 6135892
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Sarah Keen
Date completed:
September 2007
EAD encoding:
Sarah Keen, October 2007Sarah Keen, March 2008Sarah Keen, December 2009Sarah Keen, May 2010
Date modified:
RMC Staff, January 2015
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The IFHE-US serves as the coordinating pivot for all US individual and organizational members of IFHE, including the American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences (AAFCS) and others, to promote the mission and work of IFHE.
In the 1996 at the IFHE Council and Congress in Bangkok, changes to constitution and by-laws were approved allowing changes in the over all structure. These changes gave additional emphasis to programming in the five IFHE regions and provided the number of votes per country giving representation to individual members for the first time. These changes required the formation of the US structure committee resulting in the IFHE-US organization being the representative of the US members of IFHE.
IFHE-US began in a structure meeting at Lake Jackson, Florida in January 1996 and the formal creation of IFHE-US was with a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with AAFCS effective in 1999. This was followed by the formal closing of the AAFCS/IFHE Liaison Committee in June 2000.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, reports, and newsletters of IFHE-US; reports, proceedings, photographs, and other conference materials from IFHE Council meetings and Congresses. Records of the IFHE-US Development Fund.
RELATED MATERIALS
American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences records, #6578.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Governance and History
Series II. Councils, Congresses and Workshops A. Bangkok, Thailand - Council and Congress B. Sheffield, England - Council C. International Home Economics and Consumer Studies Research Conference D. Hanover, Germany - Council and Congress E. Minneapolis, Minnesota - Congress F. Mayaguez, Puerto Rico - Council G. Region of the Americas Conference - Pre-Council Workshop H. Commission on the Status of Women - Workshop I. Accra, Ghana - Council and Congress J. International Year of the Family K. Helsinki, Finland - Council L. Melbourne, Australia - World Congress
Series III. United States Development Fund
Cite As:
International Federation for Home Economics-United States records, #6760. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Videotapes. Photographs. International Year of the Family, 1994. Home economics -- International cooperation. Home economics -- Congresses.
